text.title: Testing in Go Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed when testing-go is applied incorrectly. Each entry has a symptom (`description`), root cause (`reason`), and corrective fix (in `note`).

antipattern.description: Test passes locally, flakes in CI on a `time.Sleep(100*ms)`.
antipattern.reason: Hard sleep used in place of synchronization; CI machine is slower or faster than dev box.
antipattern.note: Fix: Replace with `require.Eventually(t, predicate, timeout, tick)` or a `done` channel.

antipattern.description: Test panics with nil-pointer-deref before any `Errorf` fires.
antipattern.reason: `assert.NoError` did not stop the test; subsequent `result.Field` ran on nil.
antipattern.note: Fix: Use `require.NoError` for any error whose absence is a precondition for the rest of the test.

antipattern.description: Loop-variable bug: every parallel subtest tests the last row only.
antipattern.reason: Pre-Go-1.22 loop var captured by reference; `tc := tc` shadowing was omitted.
antipattern.note: Fix: Either upgrade to Go 1.22 (`go.mod` go 1.22) where the language change fixes this, or add `tc := tc` before `t.Run`.

antipattern.description: `go test -bench=.` shows wildly different ns/op between runs.
antipattern.reason: Single `-count=1` run, no `benchstat`, GC noise dominates.
antipattern.note: Fix: Run `-count=10` and compare with `benchstat old.txt new.txt`; never eyeball ns/op.

antipattern.description: Fuzz target panics on first run because seed corpus is empty.
antipattern.note: Fix: Add 3-5 representative `f.Add(...)` seeds covering empty, valid, and boundary inputs.
